title: Adjective
subtitle: SSC CGL Tier 1 + Tier 2 Complete Notes

definition.english: A word that modifies or describes a noun or pronoun is called an adjective.
definition.marathi: Noun किंवा Pronoun बद्दल अधिक माहिती देणाऱ्या शब्दाला Adjective म्हणतात.

examples.tag: The beautiful girl — girl बद्दल सांगते
Three boys — boys ची संख्या सांगते
This book — कोणते book ते दाखवते

cases.name: Positive Degree
cases.english: Simple form of adjective, no comparison.
cases.marathi: Adjective चे साधे रूप, कुठलीही तुलना नाही.

cases.name: Superlative Degree
cases.english: Compares more than two persons or things.
cases.marathi: दोनपेक्षा जास्त व्यक्ती किंवा वस्तूंची तुलना.

types.name: Adjective of Quality
types.english: Describes the quality or kind of a noun.
types.marathi: Noun ची गुणवत्ता किंवा प्रकार सांगते.

types.name: Adjective of Quantity
types.english: Shows how much of a thing is meant.

types.name: Adjective of Number
types.english: Shows how many persons or things are meant.

types.name: Demonstrative Adjective
types.english: Points out which person or thing is meant.
types.marathi: कोणती व्यक्ती किंवा वस्तू ते दाखवते.

types.name: Possessive Adjective
types.english: Shows possession or ownership before a noun.

types.name: Interrogative Adjective
types.english: Used with nouns to ask questions.

types.name: Distributive Adjective
types.english: Refers to each one of a number separately.

types.name: Emphasizing Adjective
types.english: Used to lay emphasis on a noun.

types.name: Exclamatory Adjective
types.english: Used with 'what' to express strong feeling.
types.marathi: What बरोबर भावना व्यक्त करण्यासाठी वापरतात.

rules.title: Comparative Degree — Between Two
rules.english: Use comparative degree when comparing exactly two persons or things. Use 'the' before comparative when comparing two.
rules.marathi: फक्त दोन व्यक्ती/वस्तूंची तुलना असेल तेव्हा Comparative Degree वापरतात.
examples: Ram is the taller of the two boys.

wrongRight.wrong: Ram is the tallest of the two boys.
wrongRight.right: Ram is the taller of the two boys.

rules.title: Superlative Degree — Among More Than Two
rules.english: Use superlative degree when comparing more than two persons or things.
rules.marathi: दोनपेक्षा जास्त व्यक्ती/वस्तूंची तुलना असेल तेव्हा Superlative Degree वापरतात.
examples: He is the best player in the team.

wrongRight.wrong: He is the better player in the team.
wrongRight.right: He is the best player in the team.

rules.title: Double Comparative / Double Superlative
rules.english: Never use 'more' with a comparative form, or 'most' with a superlative form.
rules.marathi: Comparative Form बरोबर 'more' आणि Superlative Form बरोबर 'most' वापरणे चुकीचे आहे.
examples: She is more intelligent than her brother.

wrongRight.wrong: She is more smarter than her brother.
wrongRight.right: She is smarter than her brother.

wrongRight.wrong: He is the most tallest boy.
wrongRight.right: He is the tallest boy.

rules.title: Absolute Adjectives (No Comparison)
rules.english: Certain adjectives cannot be compared as they are already at their highest degree.
rules.marathi: काही Adjectives आधीच पूर्णतम अवस्था दर्शवतात, त्यांची तुलना करता येत नाही.

wrongRight.wrong: This is a more unique design.
wrongRight.right: This is a unique design.

wrongRight.wrong: His solution is more perfect.
wrongRight.right: His solution is perfect.

rules.title: Elder / Older Rule
rules.english: Elder is used for members of the same family; Older is used for general comparison of age.
rules.marathi: Elder फक्त एकाच कुटुंबातील सदस्यांसाठी; Older सामान्य वयाच्या तुलनेसाठी वापरतात.
examples: My elder brother is a doctor.
This building is older than that one.

wrongRight.wrong: He is elder than me.
wrongRight.right: He is older than me.

wrongRight.wrong: My older brother got married.
wrongRight.right: My elder brother got married.

rules.title: Farther / Further Rule
rules.english: Farther refers to physical distance; Further means 'additional' or 'more'.
rules.marathi: Farther भौगोलिक अंतरासाठी; Further म्हणजे 'आणखी' किंवा 'अधिक' असा अर्थ.
examples: Delhi is farther from Mumbai than Pune.

wrongRight.wrong: I need further clarification about the distance.
wrongRight.right: I need farther clarification about the distance.

rules.title: Later / Latter Rule
rules.english: Later refers to time; Latter refers to the second of two things mentioned.
rules.marathi: Later वेळाशी संबंधित; Latter नुकत्याच उल्लेख केलेल्या दोनपैकी दुसऱ्याला सूचित करते.
examples: Between tea and coffee, I prefer the latter.

wrongRight.wrong: Between Sachin and Virat, the later is more aggressive.
wrongRight.right: Between Sachin and Virat, the latter is more aggressive.

rules.title: Little / A Little / The Little Rule
rules.english: Little = negligible amount (negative sense); A little = some amount (positive sense); The little = whatever small amount there is.
rules.marathi: Little = नगण्य प्रमाण (नकारात्मक); A little = थोडे (सकारात्मक); The little = जे काही थोडे आहे ते.
examples: He spent the little money he had.

wrongRight.wrong: There is little hope, so let's try.
wrongRight.right: There is a little hope, so let's try.

rules.title: Few / A Few / The Few Rule
rules.english: Few = negligible number (negative sense); A few = some number (positive sense); The few = whatever small number there is.
rules.marathi: Few = अत्यल्प संख्या (नकारात्मक); A few = काही (सकारात्मक); The few = जे काही थोडे आहेत ते.
examples: The few students who passed were rewarded.

wrongRight.wrong: Few people came to help him, so he managed.
wrongRight.right: A few people came to help him, so he managed.

rules.title: Each / Every Rule
rules.english: Each is used for two or more considered individually; Every is used for three or more considered collectively. Both take singular verbs.
rules.marathi: Each दोन किंवा अधिकांसाठी वैयक्तिकरीत्या; Every तीन किंवा अधिकांसाठी सामूहिकरीत्या वापरतात. दोन्हींनंतर Singular Verb येते.
examples: Each of the two boys was rewarded.
Every student must submit the assignment.

wrongRight.wrong: Every of the two boys was rewarded.
wrongRight.right: Each of the two boys was rewarded.

rules.title: Comparative — Exclude the Subject (Other / Else)
rules.english: When using comparative degree to compare a subject with others in the same group, use 'other' or 'else' to exclude the subject itself.
rules.marathi: एकाच गटातील तुलनेत स्वतःला वगळण्यासाठी 'other' किंवा 'else' वापरतात.
examples: Sachin is better than any other cricketer in India.

wrongRight.wrong: Sachin is better than any cricketer in India.
wrongRight.right: Sachin is better than any other cricketer in India.

rules.title: Superlative — Include the Subject (No 'other')
rules.english: When using superlative degree, do not use 'other' because the superlative already includes the subject in the comparison.
rules.marathi: Superlative Degree मध्ये 'other' वापरत नाहीत, कारण Superlative मध्ये स्वतः आधीच समाविष्ट असते.
examples: Sachin is the best cricketer in India.

wrongRight.wrong: Sachin is the best other cricketer in India.
wrongRight.right: Sachin is the best cricketer in India.

rules.title: No Sooner... Than Rule
rules.english: 'No sooner' is always followed by 'than', never by 'when' or 'then'.
rules.marathi: 'No sooner' नंतर नेहमी 'than' येतो, 'when' किंवा 'then' येत नाही.
examples: No sooner did he arrive than it started raining.

wrongRight.wrong: No sooner did he arrive when it started raining.
wrongRight.right: No sooner did he arrive than it started raining.

rules.title: Adjective Placement Rule
rules.english: Adjectives normally come before the noun they modify, but predicative adjectives come after a linking verb.
rules.marathi: Adjective सामान्यतः Noun च्या आधी येतो, पण Linking Verb नंतर येणारा Adjective Predicative असतो.

wrongRight.wrong: She is a girl beautiful.
wrongRight.right: She is a beautiful girl.

rules.title: This / That (Singular) vs These / Those (Plural)
rules.english: This and That are used with singular nouns; These and Those are used with plural nouns.
rules.marathi: This / That Singular Noun बरोबर; These / Those Plural Noun बरोबर वापरतात.

wrongRight.wrong: These kind of people are dangerous.
wrongRight.right: This kind of people is dangerous.

rules.title: Positive Degree (As...As) Rule
rules.english: In positive degree comparison using 'as...as', use the base form of the adjective (never comparative or superlative).
rules.marathi: As...as वापरताना नेहमी Positive Degree (base form) वापरतात.
examples: She is as tall as her sister.

wrongRight.wrong: She is as taller as her sister.
wrongRight.right: She is as tall as her sister.

rules.title: Prefer... to Rule
rules.english: Use Positive Degree after 'prefer...to'. Never use comparative degree after 'prefer'.
rules.marathi: Prefer...to च्या नंतर Positive Degree वापरतात. Comparative Degree वापरत नाहीत.

wrongRight.wrong: I prefer tea more than coffee.
wrongRight.right: I prefer tea to coffee.

rules.title: Senior / Junior / Superior / Inferior Rule
rules.english: These Latin comparatives take 'to' instead of 'than' and cannot be used with 'more'.
rules.marathi: Senior, Junior, Superior, Inferior हे Latin Comparatives 'than' ऐवजी 'to' वापरतात आणि 'more' बरोबर येत नाहीत.
examples: This product is inferior to that one.

wrongRight.wrong: He is more senior than me.
wrongRight.right: He is senior to me.

wrongRight.wrong: She is junior than her colleague.
wrongRight.right: She is junior to her colleague.

rules.title: The + Comparative, The + Comparative
rules.english: When two comparative degrees are used together with 'the...the', both adjectives must be in comparative form.
rules.marathi: The...the च्या संरचनेत दोन्ही Comparative Degrees वापरतात.
examples: The harder you work, the better the results.

wrongRight.wrong: The hard you work, the better the results.
wrongRight.right: The harder you work, the better the results.
